name: Stuffed Artichokes
recipeInstructions:
1. Toast pine nuts (Microwave: Spread in one layer on a microwave-safe paper towel and cook on High until lightly browned, about 2 to 4 minutes).
2. Cut artichoke stems so they are flat at the bottom and can stand in the baking dish. Snip the tips off the outer leaves.
3. Rub artichokes with the cut side of lemon.
4. Place artichokes in a microwave-safe dish and pour 1/4 cup chicken broth into the bottom.
5. Cover tightly with plastic wrap and cook on High until the bottoms are tender when pierced with the tip of a knife, about 11 minutes. Let stand covered for 3 minutes; drain.
6. When cool enough to handle, pull out the center leaves and remove the center choke.
7. Cook oil, onion, and garlic in the microwave uncovered on High for 3 minutes.
8. Add remaining ingredients and another 1/4 cup chicken broth to the onion mixture. Stir until well blended.
9. Spoon the mixture into the centers of artichokes (and between leaves if desired).
10. Pour the remaining 1/4 cup chicken broth into the bottom of the dish.
11. Place artichokes in the broth and cover with plastic wrap. Cook on High until heated through, about 4 minutes.
